Getting Started with onet2r

Source: vignettes/getting-started.Rmd
getting-started.Rmd
library(onet2r)
library(dplyr)
#> 
#> Attaching package: 'dplyr'
#> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
#> 
#>     filter, lag
#> The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
#> 
#>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union

has_onet_key <- nzchar(Sys.getenv("ONET_API_KEY"))
run_live <- has_onet_key && identical(Sys.getenv("ONET2R_RUN_LIVE_VIGNETTES"), "true")

sample_oews <- system.file(
  "extdata",
  "oews-national-sample.csv",
  package = "onet2r"
)

archive_302 <- system.file(
  "extdata",
  "onet-mini",
  "db_30_2_text",
  package = "onet2r"
)

archive_303 <- system.file(
  "extdata",
  "onet-mini",
  "db_30_3_text",
  package = "onet2r"
)

onet2r helps you move from occupation search to analysis-ready O*NET tables, archived O*NET releases, and BLS OEWS wage and employment context. Current O*NET Web Services calls require a free API key from the O*NET developer portal. Store it in .Renviron as ONET_API_KEY=your-api-key-here so you do not put secrets in scripts.

The live Web Services examples are opt-in for package builds, because CRAN and most CI environments should not depend on an external API. The rest of this article uses local files through actual onet2r functions, so every displayed table is computed when the vignette is knitted.

tibble::tibble(
  setting = c("ONET_API_KEY configured", "live vignette API calls enabled"),
  value = c(has_onet_key, run_live)
)
#> # A tibble: 2 × 2
#>   setting                         value
#>   <chr>                           <lgl>
#> 1 ONET_API_KEY configured         TRUE 
#> 2 live vignette API calls enabled FALSE

Search the Current O*NET API

When live calls are enabled, the first step is usually onet_search(), followed by a detail endpoint such as onet_skills() or onet_abilities().

if (run_live) {
  onet_search("software developer", start = 1, end = 5)
} else {
  tibble::tibble(
    live_api_example = "skipped",
    reason = "Set ONET_API_KEY and ONET2R_RUN_LIVE_VIGNETTES=true to run onet_search()."
  )
}
#> # A tibble: 1 × 2
#>   live_api_example reason                                                       
#>   <chr>            <chr>                                                        
#> 1 skipped          Set ONET_API_KEY and ONET2R_RUN_LIVE_VIGNETTES=true to run o…
if (run_live) {
  onet_skills("15-1252.00", start = 1, end = 5)
} else {
  tibble::tibble(
    live_api_example = "skipped",
    reason = "Set ONET_API_KEY and ONET2R_RUN_LIVE_VIGNETTES=true to run onet_skills()."
  )
}
#> # A tibble: 1 × 2
#>   live_api_example reason                                                       
#>   <chr>            <chr>                                                        
#> 1 skipped          Set ONET_API_KEY and ONET2R_RUN_LIVE_VIGNETTES=true to run o…

Read an Archived O*NET Table

The Web Services API serves the current release. Historical work uses the downloadable archive tables. onet_archive_read() normalizes those archive files into tibbles with stable columns.

abilities <- onet_archive_read(
  "30.3",
  "Abilities",
  path = archive_303,
  release_date = "2026-05-01"
)

head(abilities, 8)
#> # A tibble: 7 × 18
#>   release_version release_date soc_vintage domain   onet_soc_code soc_code title
#>   <chr>           <date>       <fct>       <chr>    <chr>         <chr>    <chr>
#> 1 30.3            2026-05-01   2019        Abiliti… 15-1252.00    15-1252  Soft…
#> 2 30.3            2026-05-01   2019        Abiliti… 15-1252.00    15-1252  Soft…
#> 3 30.3            2026-05-01   2019        Abiliti… 29-1141.00    29-1141  Regi…
#> 4 30.3            2026-05-01   2019        Abiliti… 29-1141.00    29-1141  Regi…
#> 5 30.3            2026-05-01   2019        Abiliti… 11-1011.00    11-1011  Chie…
#> 6 30.3            2026-05-01   2019        Abiliti… 11-1011.00    11-1011  Chie…
#> 7 30.3            2026-05-01   2019        Abiliti… 41-1011.00    41-1011  Firs…
#> # ℹ 11 more variables: element_id <chr>, element_name <chr>, scale_id <fct>,
#> #   data_value <dbl>, n <int>, standard_error <dbl>, lower_ci_bound <dbl>,
#> #   upper_ci_bound <dbl>, recommend_suppress <chr>, source_date <date>,
#> #   domain_source <fct>

Add Labor-Market Context

BLS OEWS estimates add employment and wage scale to O*NET occupation rows. onet_oews_national() reads the OEWS file, and onet_join_oews() joins by a normalized SOC code.

oews <- onet_oews_national(2024, path = sample_oews)

occupations <- abilities |>
  distinct(code = onet_soc_code, title)

occupation_wages <- onet_join_oews(occupations, oews = oews)

occupation_wages |>
  select(code, title, soc_code, tot_emp, a_median)
#> # A tibble: 4 × 5
#>   code       title                                     soc_code tot_emp a_median
#>   <chr>      <chr>                                     <chr>      <dbl>    <dbl>
#> 1 15-1252.00 Software Developers                       15-1252  1847900   133080
#> 2 29-1141.00 Registered Nurses                         29-1141  3175400    93070
#> 3 11-1011.00 Chief Executives                          11-1011   211230   206680
#> 4 41-1011.00 First-Line Supervisors of Retail Sales W… 41-1011       NA       NA

Summarize O*NET Rows with Employment Weights

onet_weighted_summary() lets you summarize descriptor rows by employment or wage context. Here it computes employment-weighted ability means from actual archive rows and the bundled OEWS sample.

onet_weighted_summary(
  abilities,
  group = c("element_id", "element_name"),
  value = "data_value",
  occupation_code = "onet_soc_code",
  oews = oews
)
#> # A tibble: 2 × 7
#>   element_id element_name     n_records n_occupations total_weight weighted_mean
#>   <chr>      <chr>                <int>         <int>        <dbl>         <dbl>
#> 1 1.A.1.a.1  Oral Comprehens…         3             3      5234530          4.57
#> 2 1.A.1.b.1  Problem Sensiti…         3             3      5234530          4.73
#> # ℹ 1 more variable: wage_weighted_mean <dbl>

Compare Two Archive Releases

For historical analysis, build a panel, reconcile adjacent releases, and inspect comparability flags before interpreting changes.

panel <- onet_panel(
  "Abilities",
  versions = c("30.2", "30.3"),
  scale = "IM",
  archives = c(`30.2` = archive_302, `30.3` = archive_303),
  release_dates = c(`30.2` = "2026-02-01", `30.3` = "2026-05-01")
)

changes <- onet_panel_reconcile(
  panel,
  bridge = onet_crosswalk_bridge("2019", "2019")
)

changes |>
  select(
    to_soc_code,
    element_name,
    from_value,
    to_value,
    value_change,
    change_type,
    safely_comparable
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(abs(value_change))) |>
  head(8)
#> # A tibble: 7 × 7
#>   to_soc_code element_name        from_value to_value value_change change_type  
#>   <chr>       <chr>                    <dbl>    <dbl>        <dbl> <fct>        
#> 1 29-1141     Problem Sensitivity       4.6      4.9         0.300 recode_or_re…
#> 2 15-1252     Oral Comprehension        4.12     4.35        0.230 real_update  
#> 3 41-1011     Oral Comprehension        4        4.15        0.150 real_update  
#> 4 11-1011     Oral Comprehension        4.38     4.5         0.120 real_update  
#> 5 15-1252     Problem Sensitivity       4.5      4.5         0     stale_carryf…
#> 6 29-1141     Oral Comprehension        4.71     4.71        0     resampled_st…
#> 7 11-1011     Problem Sensitivity       4.22     4.22        0     stale_carryf…
#> # ℹ 1 more variable: safely_comparable <lgl>

Next Steps

  • Read vignette("longitudinal-onet-background", package = "onet2r") before interpreting cross-release changes.
  • Read vignette("longitudinal-archives", package = "onet2r") to assemble and reconcile archive panels.
  • Read vignette("oews-wage-context", package = "onet2r") to add BLS wage and employment estimates.
